name: Vaguely Indonesian Stir-Fry
recipeIngredient: 1 Head of broccoli, cut into florets (about 1 cup)
1 medium Onion, quartered and cut into pieces about 1/2 inch thick
1 Carrot, sliced into rounds 1/4 inch thick
1/2 lb Oyster mushrooms, sliced into strips
1 Tbsp Corn starch dissolved in 1/2 cup warm water
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat your wok, add a splash of oil and let heat.
2. Quickly stir-fry the carrot for about 1 minute.
3. Add broccoli and stir for another minute.
4. Add onion and stir until cooked for about 1 minute.
5. Add the marinade, stir one minute.
6. Add the peanut sauce, stir one more minute.
7. Stir in the cornstarch mixture and cook until it thickens.
8. To serve, place a mound of jasmine rice on each plate.
9. Pile the stir-fry on one side of the rice.
10. Arrange two tempeh wedges around the other side.
11. Garnish the tempeh with garlic chunks from roasting.
12. Pour any remaining sauce over the tempeh.
